answer_key.q29: F. Opportunities for all round prevalent applications

passages.title: Texting the Television
passages.source_html:
Texting the Television 


A 


There was a time when any self-respecting television show, particularly one aimed at a young audience, had to have an e-mail address. But on Europe’s TV screens, such addresses are increasingly being pushed aside in favour of telephone numbers to which viewers can send text messages from their mobile phones. And no wonder: according to research about to be published by Gartner, a consultancy, text messaging has recently overtaken Internet use in Europe. One of the fastest-growing uses of text messaging, moreover, is interacting with television. Gartner’s figures show that 20% of teenagers in France, 11% in Britain and 9% in Germany have sent messages in response to TV shows.


B 


This has much to do with the boom in ‘reality TV’ shows, such as ‘Big Brother’, in which viewers’ votes decide the outcome. Most reality shows now allow text-message voting, and in some cases, such as the most recent series of ‘Big Brother’ in Norway, the majority of votes are cast in this way. But there is more to TV-texting than voting. News shows encourage viewers to send in comments; games shows allow viewers to compete; music shows take requests by text message; and broadcasters operate on-screen chartrooms. People tend to have their mobiles with them on the sofa, so ‘it’s a very natural form of interaction,’ says Adam Daum of Gartner.


C


It can also be very lucrative, since mobile operators charge premium rates for messages to particular numbers. The most recent British series of ‘Big Brother’, for example, generated 5.4m text-message votes and £1.35m ($2.1m) in revenue. According to a report from Van Dusseldorp & Partners, a consultancy based in Amsterdam, the German edition of MTV’s ‘Videoclash’, which invites viewers to vote for one of two rival videos, generates up to 40,000 messages an hour, each costing euro0.30 ($0.29). A text contest alongside the Belgian quiz show ‘I Against 100’ attracted 10,000 players in a month, each of whom paid euro 0.50 per question in an eight-round contest. In Spain, a cryptic-crossword clue is displayed before the evening news broadcast; viewers are invited to text in their answers at a cost of euro 1, for a chance to win a euro300 prize. On a typical day, 6,000 people take part. TV-related text messaging now accounts for an appreciable share of mobile operators’ data revenues. In July, a British operator, mmO2, reported better-than-expected financial results, thanks to the flood of messages caused by ‘Big Brother’. Operators typically take 40-50% of the revenue from each message, with the rest divided between the broadcaster, the programme maker and the firm providing the message-processing system. Text-message revenues are already a vital element of the business model for many shows. Inevitably, there is grumbling that the operators take too much of the pie. Endemol, the Netherlands-based production company behind ‘Big Brother’ and many other reality TV shows has started building its own database of mobile-phone users. The next step will be to establish direct billing relationships with them, and bypass the operators.


D 


Why has the union of television and text message suddenly proved so successful? One important factor is the availability of special four-, five- or six-digit numbers, called ‘shortcodes’.Each operator controls its own shortcodes, and only relatively recently have operators realised that it makes sense to co-operate and offer shortcodes that work across all networks. The availability of such common shortcodes was a breakthrough, says Lars Becker of Flytxt, a mobile-marketing firm, since shortcodes are far easier to remember when flashed up on the screen.


E 


The operators’ decision to co-operate in order to expand the market is part of a broader trend, observes Katrina Bond of Analysys, a consultancy. Faced with a choice between protecting their margins and allowing a new medium to emerge, operators have always chosen the first. WAP, a technology for reading cut-down web pages on mobile phones, failed because operators were reluctant to share revenue with content providers. Having learnt their lesson, operators are changing their tune. In France, one operator, Orange, has even gone so far as to publish a rate card for text-message revenue-sharing, a degree of transparency that would once have been unthinkable.


F 


At a recent conference organised by Van Dusseldorp & Partners, Han Weegink of CMG, a firm that provides text-message infrastructure, noted that all this is subtly changing the nature of television. Rather than presenting content to viewers, an increasing number of programmes involve content that reacts to the viewer’s input. That was always the promise of interactive TV, of course. Interactive TV was supposed to revolve around fancy set-top boxes that plug directly into the television. But that approach has a number of drawbacks, says Mr Daum. It is expensive to develop and test software for multiple and incompatible types of set-top box, and the market penetration, at 40% or less, is lower than that for mobile phones, which are now owned by around 85% of Europeans. Also, mobile-phone applications can be quickly developed and set up. ‘You can get to market faster, and with fewer grasping intermediaries,’ says Mr Daum. Providers of set-top box technology are adding text-messaging capabilities to their products.


G 


The success of TV-related texting is a reminder of how easily an elaborate technology can be unexpectedly overtaken by a simpler, lower-tech approach. It does not mean that the traditional approach to interactive TV is doomed: indeed, it demonstrates that there is strong demand for interactive services. People, it seems, really do want to do more than just stare at the screen. If nothing else, couch potatoes like to exercise their thumbs.

groups.title: Questions 28-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 28-32 


The passage has seven paragraphs A-G. Choose the correct heading for paragraphS A-G from the list below. Write the correct number i-ix in boxes 28-32 on your answer sheet.




Options (Word Bank):
A. An existed critical system into operating in a new wa
B. Overview of a fast growing business
C. Profitable games are gaining more concerns
D. Netherlands takes the leading role
E. A new perspective towards sharing the business opportunities
F. Opportunities for all round prevalent applications
G. Revenue gains and bonus share
H. The simpler technology prevails over complex ones
I. Set-top box provider changed their mind
28
正确答案： 28 B. Overview of a fast growing business  |  29 F. Opportunities for all round prevalent applications  |  30 G. Revenue gains and bonus share  |  31 A. An existed critical system into operating in a new wa  |  32 E. A new perspective towards sharing the business opportunities
Question 28

原文答案句：And no wonder: according to research about to be published by Gartner, a consultancy, text messaging has recently overtaken Internet use in Europe. One of the fastest-growing uses of text messaging, moreover, is interacting with television. 

对应段落：A段

审题：题目要求为A段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过阅读A段，可以发现段落主要介绍了电视观众从使用电子邮件与电视互动转向使用短信，并提到短信在欧洲的使用已经超过互联网，尤其是与电视互动的短信增长迅速。

解题思路：A段首先提到电视节目曾经需要电子邮件地址，但现在更多地使用电话号码让观众发送短信。接着指出短信在欧洲的使用已经超过互联网，并且与电视互动的短信是增长最快的用途之一。因此，A段的主要内容是关于短信作为一种快速增长的与电视互动方式的概述。选项B"Overview of a fast growing business"（概述一项快速增长的业务）最能概括A段的内容。


Question 29

原文答案句：This has much to do with the boom in ‘reality TV’ shows, such as ‘Big Brother’, in which viewers’ votes decide the outcome. Most reality shows now allow text-message voting, and in some cases, such as the most recent series of ‘Big Brother’ in Norway, the majority of votes are cast in this way. 

对应段落：B段

审题：题目要求为B段选择正确的标题。

定位关键信息：根据题目定位到文章的B段。

解题思路：B段主要讲述了短信互动与电视结合的现象，特别是与真人秀节目的联系，如"Big Brother"等节目中观众通过短信投票决定结果。段落中还提到了其他类型的电视节目，如新闻节目鼓励观众发送评论、游戏节目让观众参与竞猜等。这些内容都围绕着"短信在电视互动的多种应用场景"展开，因此最符合的标题是F"Opportunities for all round prevalent applications"有机会全方位普及应用


Question 30

原文答案句：It can also be very lucrative, since mobile operators charge premium rates for messages to particular numbers. 

对应段落：C段

审题：题目要求为C段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过阅读C段内容，发现段落主要围绕短信互动产生的经济效益展开，包括收入来源、运营商分成等。

解题思路：C段首句就提到，这也能带来丰厚利润，因为移动运营商会对特定号码的短信收取高额费用。后又详细列举了短信互动在多个电视节目中的盈利情况，如"Big Brother"产生的收入、"Videoclash"的短信费用等，并提到短信收入已成为许多节目的重要商业模式。因此，C段的核心内容是短信互动的盈利模式，与标题G"Revenue gains and bonus share"（收入增长和利润分配）完全吻合。


Question 31

原文答案句：Each operator controls its own shortcodes, and only relatively recently have operators realised that it makes sense to co-operate and offer shortcodes that work across all networks. The availability of such common shortcodes was a breakthrough, says Lars Becker of Flytxt, a mobile-marketing firm, since shortcodes are far easier to remember when flashed up on the screen.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目要求为D段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过阅读D段，可以发现段落主要讨论了“shortcodes”（短代码）的重要性和跨网络使用短代码的突破。

解题思路：D段首先提到“shortcodes”是成功的重要因素之一，接着详细描述了这些短代码的特点（如四位、五位或六位数字），各运营商原本各自管理专属短代码，直到近年才意识到合作开发通用短代码的必要性。移动营销公司Flytxt的拉尔斯·贝克尔指出，这种通用短代码的推出堪称重大突破—以及运营商如何合作提供跨网络的短代码。因此，D段的核心内容是关于“shortcodes”的新突破，这与选项A“An existed critical system into operating in a new way”现存的一个重要系统以新方式投入运行，完全吻合。


Question 32

原文答案句：The operators’ decision to co-operate in order to expand the market is part of a broader trend, observes Katrina Bond of Analysys, a consultancy. 

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求为E段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：根据E段内容，重点在于移动运营商从最初保护自身利润到后来选择合作以拓展市场的转变。

解题思路：E段首句就提到，运营商为拓展市场而选择合作的决策，正是行业整体趋势的缩影。讲述了移动运营商在面临保护利润和推动新媒介发展之间的选择，最终选择了合作以扩大市场。这与选项E“A new perspective towards sharing the business opportunities”（共享商业机遇的新思路）高度吻合，因此选择该选项作为E段的标题。


Questions 33-35


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D. Write your answers in boxes 33-35 on your answer sheet.


33

In Europe, a consultancy suggested that young audiences spend more money on

正确答案： A. thumbing text message.

原文答案句：And no wonder: according to research about to be published by Gartner, a consultancy, text messaging has recently overtaken Internet use in Europe. One of the fastest-growing uses of text messaging, moreover, is interacting with television. Gartner’s figures show that 20% of teenagers in France, 11% in Britain and 9% in Germany have sent messages in response to TV shows.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问在欧洲，一家咨询公司调查显示年轻观众会花更多的钱在什么上。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"Europe"、"consultancy"和"young audiences"定位到A段，其中提到了Gartner这家咨询公司以及年轻人使用短信的情况。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子，Gartner这家咨询公司指出短信在欧洲的使用已经超过了互联网，并且年轻人通过短信与电视互动的比例很高。因此，可以推断出年轻人花费更多的钱在发送短信上，即选项A。


34

What happened when some TV show invited audience to participate?

正确答案： D. Their participation could change the result

原文答案句：Most reality shows now allow text-message voting, and in some cases, such as the most recent series of ‘Big Brother’ in Norway, the majority of votes are cast in this way. But there is more to TV-texting than voting. News shows encourage viewers to send in comments; games shows allow viewers to compete; music shows take requests by text message; and broadcasters operate on-screen chartrooms. 

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是当一些电视节目邀请观众参与时会发生什么。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"invited audience to participate"在文章中定位，找到B段中提到观众投票决定结果的句子。

解题思路：根据原文，如今大多数真人秀节目都支持短信投票，新闻节目鼓励观众留言评论，游戏节目让观众参与竞猜，音乐节目接受短信点歌，电视台还会在屏幕上设置排行榜。可知，观众参与（投票）可以决定节目的结果，这与选项D"他们的参与可以改变结果"完全一致。其他选项A、B、C在原文中均未提及或与原文内容不符。因此，正确答案是D。


35

Interactive TV change their mind of concentrating set-top box but switched to

正确答案： C. build a embedded message platform.

原文答案句：Providers of set-top box technology are adding text-messaging capabilities to their products.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问互动电视不再关注机顶盒装置，转而转向了什么。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"Interactive TV"和"set-top box"定位到F段，该段提到互动电视原本依赖于机顶盒技术，但存在缺点。

解题思路：F段后半部分提到，机顶盒技术存在开发成本高、市场渗透率低等问题，而移动手机应用开发快、市场接受度高。因此，互动电视技术提供商开始在其产品中加入短信功能（text-messaging capabilities），即构建嵌入式短信平台。选项C"build a embedded message platform"与原文意思一致。其他选项A、B、D在原文中均未提及。


Questions 36-40 


Use the information in the passage to match the people (listed A-E) with opinions or deeds below. Write the appropriate letters A-E in boxes 36-40 on your answer sheet.


A Lars Becker Flytxt 

B Katrina Bond of Analysys 

C Endemol 

D CMG 

E mmO2 

F Gartner 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
36

36 To offer mobile phone message technology 
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：At a recent conference organised by Van Dusseldorp & Partners, Han Weegink of CMG, a firm that provides text-message infrastructure, noted that all this is subtly changing the nature of television. 

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“提供手机短信技术的公司是哪家？”

定位关键信息：根据题干中的关键词“mobile phone message technology”定位到文章中F段，其中提到“Han Weegink of CMG, a firm that provides text-message infrastructure”，表明CMG公司提供短信基础设施技术。

解题思路：根据原文句子可以确定，CMG公司提供的是短信基础设施服务，即与手机短信相关的技术，因此答案为D。
37

37 Earned considerable amount of money through a famous program
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：In July, a British operator, mmO2, reported better-than-expected financial results, thanks to the flood of messages caused by ‘Big Brother’. 

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是哪家公司通过一个著名的节目赚了大量的钱。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是“Earned considerable amount of money through a famous program”，在文章中定位到C段，找到与“mmO2”相关的句子。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子，今年7月，英国运营商mmO2公布了好于预期的财务业绩，这要归功于“老大哥”引发的大量信息。可以确定mmO2是“Big Brother”的合作运营商，并且通过这个节目赚了大量的钱。因此，答案是E。
38

38 Shortcodes are convenient to remember when turn up
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：The availability of such common shortcodes was a breakthrough, says Lars Becker of Flytxt, a mobile-marketing firm, since shortcodes are far easier to remember when flashed up on the screen.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目询问“短代码出现时便于记忆”，需要匹配发表此观点的人。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词"shortcodes"和"remember when turn up"（易记忆当出现时）可以定位到D段中Lars Becker关于shortcodes的描述。

解题思路：原文D段提到Lars Becker明确指出shortcodes因为"far easier to remember when flashed up on the screen"（当在屏幕上闪烁时更容易记忆），这与题目描述完全一致。因此，发表此观点的人是A。
39

39 To build their own mobile phone operating applications
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Endemol, the Netherlands-based production company behind ‘Big Brother’ and many other reality TV shows has started building its own database of mobile-phone users. The next step will be to establish direct billing relationships with them, and bypass the operators.

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问的是哪家公司正在建立自己的手机应用程序。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"build their own mobile phone operating applications"定位到C段，找到与建立手机用户数据库相关的句子。  

解题思路：原文中提到Endemol公司正在建立自己的手机用户数据库，下一步将直接与用户结算费用，绕过运营商。这与题干中"build their own mobile phone operating applications"相符，因此答案为C。
40

40 It is easy for people to send messages in an interactive TV
✅ 正确答案：F

原文答案句：People tend to have their mobiles with them on the sofa, so ‘it’s a very natural form of interaction,’ says Adam Daum of Gartner.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是“人们很容易在互动电视中发送信息”，需要找到对应人物或公司的观点或行为。

定位关键信息：通过“easy for people to send messages”定位到F段中关于移动应用程序开发的内容。

解题思路：B段提到，Gartner的Adam Daum表示，人们往往在沙发上带着手机，所以“这是一种非常自然的互动形式”。说明使用手机发送信息参与电视节目互动简便快捷，因此对应Gartner公司Mr Daum的观点，选择F。
